name: MS-QRD-006: Privilege Escalation — Sensitive Privilege Use and Token Manipulation
description: Detects privilege escalation attempts by monitoring Windows Security events related to sensitive privilege assignments (EventID 4672), special logon events, and token manipulation. Identifies accounts that gain elevated privileges outside of expected patterns — specifically non-service accounts acquiring SeDebugPrivilege, SeTcbPrivilege, SeImpersonatePrivilege, or SeAssignPrimaryTokenPrivilege. These privileges are commonly abused by tools like Mimikatz, Juicy Potato, PrintSpoofer, and other local privilege escalation exploits.
author: MilliSec Blue Team — Rihad Ebilov

qradar.aql: SELECT sourceip AS "Source IP", username AS "Account", LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS "Log Source", CATEGORYNAME(category) AS "Event Category", QIDNAME(qid) AS "Event Name", COUNT(*) AS "Event Count", MIN(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "First Seen", MAX(DATEFORMAT(starttime, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')) AS "Last Seen" FROM events WHERE (CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%privilege%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%token%' OR CATEGORYNAME(category) ILIKE '%escalation%' OR QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%special logon%' OR QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%sensitive privilege%' OR QIDNAME(qid) ILIKE '%4672%') AND username IS NOT NULL AND username != '-' AND username != 'SYSTEM' AND username != 'LOCAL SERVICE' AND username != 'NETWORK SERVICE' AND NOT (username ILIKE '%$' AND username ILIKE '%svc%') GROUP BY sourceip, username, LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid), CATEGORYNAME(category), QIDNAME(qid) HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3 ORDER BY "Event Count" DESC LAST 10 MINUTES
qradar.offense_relevance: Non-service accounts acquiring sensitive privileges (SeDebugPrivilege, SeTcbPrivilege, SeImpersonatePrivilege) is a high-confidence indicator of privilege escalation. SeDebugPrivilege specifically is required by Mimikatz to access LSASS memory. SeTcbPrivilege and SeAssignPrimaryTokenPrivilege are targeted by Potato-family exploits (Juicy Potato, Sweet Potato, PrintSpoofer). Correlate with Splunk rule MS-SPL-008 (Mimikatz detection) for full attack chain visibility.
response_actions: Immediately investigate the account shown — determine if it is a legitimate admin or compromised user
Review running processes on source host for known exploitation tools
Check for newly created services or scheduled tasks with SYSTEM privileges
Revoke the sensitive privilege assignment if unauthorized
Force logoff and password reset for the affected account
Collect memory dump from affected host before killing suspicious processes
qradar.false_positive_notes: Legitimate administrator accounts performing privileged operations. Security scanning tools running under admin context. Backup agents that require SeBackupPrivilege. Domain join operations. Reduce false positives by maintaining a QRadar Reference Set of known privileged service accounts and adding exclusion filter.
qradar.tuning_notes: The WHERE clause excludes SYSTEM, LOCAL SERVICE, NETWORK SERVICE, and accounts ending in '$' (computer accounts) or containing 'svc' (service accounts). For more precision, create a QRadar Reference Set 'Authorized_Privileged_Accounts' containing known admin accounts and add: AND username NOT IN (SELECT value FROM REFERENCETABLE('Authorized_Privileged_Accounts')). Threshold of 3 events in 10 minutes balances sensitivity vs noise.
